name: MyFatoorah Gulf Region Payment Gateway API
what_it_does: MyFatoorah Gulf region payment gateway REST API for merchants across Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, UAE, Bahrain, Qatar, Oman, Jordan, and Egypt to accept local payment methods including KNET (Kuwait), mada (Saudi Arabia), Benefit (Bahrain), NAPS (Qatar), card, Apple Pay, Samsung Pay, STCPay, and bank transfer through a single unified API integration covering 8+ GCC and MENA countries. Enables AI agents to manage multi-country GCC payment checkout for Gulf region e-commerce automation, handle KNET debit payment for Kuwait consumer checkout automation, access mada debit for Saudi consumer payment automation, retrieve Benefit payment for Bahrain consumer automation, manage Apple Pay integration for Gulf premium consumer automation, handle STCPay for Saudi telecom wallet automation, access payment status for Gulf transaction verification automation, retrieve refund processing for GCC payment reversal automation, manage webhook notification for Gulf payment event automation, and integrate MyFatoorah with Gulf e-commerce, government services, and enterprise platforms for end-to-end GCC payment automation.
use_cases: Accepting KNET, mada, and Benefit payments from GCC consumers through single API integration agents
Processing Apple Pay and STCPay for Gulf premium consumer checkout automation agents
Managing multi-country payment acceptance across Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and UAE from regional agents
Receiving payment webhooks for Gulf marketplace and government service fulfillment automation agents
not_for: Markets outside GCC/MENA (MyFatoorah is Gulf and Middle East focused)
High-volume global payment processing (use Stripe or Adyen for global enterprise scale)
Cryptocurrency payments (MyFatoorah is traditional payment methods only)
best_when: A Gulf region merchant or enterprise wanting AI agents to accept all local GCC payment methods (KNET, mada, Benefit, NAPS, STCPay) through MyFatoorah's unified API covering 8+ countries across Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and UAE.
avoid_when: COUNTRY-SPECIFIC GATEWAY CONFIGURATION: MyFatoorah gateway configuration differs per country (Kuwait KNET, Saudi mada, Bahrain Benefit); automated single global configuration assumption creates wrong payment method for country; automated must configure country-specific payment methods. LOCAL CURRENCY REQUIRED: MyFatoorah processes in local currency (KWD, SAR, AED, BHD, QAR, OMR); automated USD assumption creates currency mismatch; automated must use local currency code per country. 3D SECURE IS MANDATORY: Gulf regional card payments require 3D Secure OTP; automated 3DS bypass creates systematic decline; automated checkout must implement 3DS redirect flow. MERCHANT REGISTRATION PER COUNTRY: MyFatoorah merchant account may require country-specific business registration for local payment methods; automated global single registration assumption creates local payment method ineligibility.

auth.notes: MyFatoorah uses bearer token API key for authentication. REST API with JSON. Kuwait City, Kuwait HQ. Founded 2014. Products: KNET, mada, Benefit, NAPS, card, Apple Pay, Samsung Pay, STCPay, Fawry, bank transfer. SDKs: PHP, .NET, Node.js, Python, Java. PCI DSS. GDPR. Compliant with CBK, SAMA, CBUAE, CBB, QCB. 8+ countries. Serves Gulf e-commerce, government, healthcare. Competes with Tap Payments and HyperPay for GCC payment aggregation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Sandbox testing environment free; production requires MyFatoorah merchant account
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-transaction: local debit (KNET, mada) negotiated flat; card processing 2-3%; Apple Pay 2-3%
pricing.notes: Kuwait City KW. Founded 2014. PCI DSS. GCC multi-currency. Per-transaction pricing by method and country.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; payment and refund APIs throttled; webhooks for payment events

known_agent_gotchas: PAYMENT METHOD LIST IS COUNTRY-SPECIFIC: MyFatoorah payment methods available vary by merchant country (KNET for Kuwait, mada for Saudi); automated universal method list assumption creates method_not_available in wrong country; automated must call initiate payment to get available methods for consumer country

LOCAL CURRENCY REQUIRED PER COUNTRY: MyFatoorah transactions must use local currency (KWD for Kuwait, SAR for Saudi, AED for UAE); automated USD pricing creates currency code error; automated must use country-specific currency

3DS REDIRECT REQUIRED FOR CARDS: Gulf card payments require 3D Secure OTP via bank SMS; automated non-3DS card processing creates systematic decline; automated checkout must implement 3DS redirect before payment confirmation
BEARER TOKEN IN AUTHORIZATION HEADER: MyFatoorah uses Bearer token authentication; automated API key as query parameter creates 401; automated must use Authorization: Bearer {token} header format

WEBHOOK VALIDATION REQUIRED: MyFatoorah sends payment notification webhooks; automated unverified webhook processing creates spoofed payment confirmation risk; automated must verify webhook origin from MyFatoorah IP range
agent_readiness.security_notes: GCC payment gateway. CBK, SAMA, CBUAE, PCI-DSS. Bearer API key. KW/SA/AE. Payment and card data.

security_score_components.security_notes: GCC payment gateway. Multi-country regulated. PCI-DSS. Bearer API key. Gulf region. Payment and card data.
